items.en: A training algorithm AA specifies how parameters are updated given data. Formally, AA maps current parameters and a training example (or batch) to updated parameters: θi=A​(θi−1,(xi,yi))\theta_{i}=A(\theta_{i-1},(x_{i},y_{i})). The algorithm AA encompasses the choice of optimizer, learning rate schedule, and all other hyperparameters that affect the sequence of parameter updates. Given initial parameters θ0\theta_{0} and dataset DD, the algorithm produces a sequence of parameters θ0,θ1,…,θn\theta_{0},\theta_{1},\ldots,\theta_{n} where θi\theta_{i} denotes the parameters after training on the first ii examples.
items.zh: 训练算法 A 指定了如何根据数据更新参数。形式上，A 将当前参数和一个训练示例（或批次）映射到更新后的参数：θi = A(θi-1, (xi, yi))。算法 A 包括优化器、学习率调度以及所有影响参数更新序列的超参数的选择。给定初始参数 θ0 和数据集 D，算法产生参数序列 θ0, θ1, …, θn，其中 θi 表示在前 i 个示例上训练后的参数。

items.en: Throughout, θ∗\theta^{*} denotes the parameters after training completes (the final trained model parameters, considerate of any early stopping condition), which are not necessarily the globally optimal parameters. When we need to refer to optimal parameters, we write θopt=argminθ​L​(θ)\theta_{\text{opt}}=\mathrm{argmin}_{\theta}L(\theta).
items.zh: 全文，θ* 表示训练完成后的参数（最终训练模型参数，考虑任何早停条件），不一定是全局最优参数。当我们需要引用最优参数时，写作 θopt = argminθ L(θ)。

items.en: The cross-entropy loss admits a fundamental interpretation in terms of information theory. Shannon’s source coding theorem establishes that if we wish to communicate symbols drawn from a distribution PP using a code designed for distribution QQ, the expected codelength per symbol is H​(P,Q)=−𝔼x∼P​[log⁡Q​(x)]H(P,Q)=-\mathbb{E}_{x\sim P}[\log Q(x)], the cross-entropy between PP and QQ. This quantity is minimized when Q=PQ=P, in which case it equals the entropy H​(P)H(P).
items.zh: 交叉熵损失在信息论中具有基本解释。香农信源编码定理表明，如果我们希望使用为分布 Q 设计的编码来传达从分布 P 中抽取的符号，每个符号的期望编码长度为 H(P, Q) = -𝔼x∼P[log Q(x)]，即 P 和 Q 之间的交叉熵。当 Q = P 时该量最小，此时等于熵 H(P)。

items.en: For a single realization, the instantaneous codelength to encode outcome yy using a code based on distribution QQ is −log⁡Q​(y)-\log Q(y). When a model assigns probability pθ​(y∣x)p_{\theta}(y\mid x) to the true label yy given input xx, the cross-entropy loss ℓ​(θ;x,y)=−log⁡pθ​(y∣x)\ell(\theta;x,y)=-\log p_{\theta}(y\mid x) is precisely the number of nats (or bits, with appropriate base) required to encode yy using the model’s predictive distribution as the coding scheme.
items.zh: 对于单个实现，使用基于分布 Q 的编码对结果 y 进行编码的瞬时编码长度为 -log Q(y)。当模型对给定输入 x 的真实标签 y 分配概率 pθ(y|x) 时，交叉熵损失 ℓ(θ; x, y) = -log pθ(y|x) 正是使用模型的预测分布作为编码方案对 y 进行编码所需的奈特（或比特，使用适当底数）数。

items.en: This interpretation transforms questions about learning into questions about compression. A model that predicts well, assigning high probability to correct labels, is equivalently a model that can compactly encode those labels. Improvements in prediction correspond directly to reductions in codelength.
items.zh: 这种解释将关于学习的问题转化为关于压缩的问题。一个预测良好、对正确标签分配高概率的模型，等价于一个能够紧凑编码这些标签的模型。预测的改进直接对应于编码长度的减少。

items.en: Prequential (predictive sequential) coding provides a framework for encoding a sequence of observations without requiring a separate description of the model used for encoding. The fundamental principle, developed by Dawid (1984) and refined in the context of minimum description length (Rissanen, 1978), is to encode each observation using the predictive distribution conditioned on all previous observations, then update the predictor before encoding the next observation.
items.zh: 序贯（预测序列）编码提供了一个框架，用于编码观测序列，而无需单独描述用于编码的模型。由 Dawid (1984) 发展并在最小描述长度（Rissanen, 1978）背景下完善的基本原则是，使用基于所有先前观测的条件预测分布编码每个观测，然后在编码下一个观测之前更新预测器。

items.en: Consider encoding a sequence of labels y1,…,yny_{1},\ldots,y_{n} given corresponding inputs x1,…,xnx_{1},\ldots,x_{n}. In prequential coding, we proceed as follows: encode y1y_{1} using the initial model’s prediction pθ0​(y∣x1)p_{\theta_{0}}(y\mid x_{1}), incurring codelength ℓ​(θ0;x1,y1)\ell(\theta_{0};x_{1},y_{1}); update parameters to θ1=A​(θ0,(x1,y1))\theta_{1}=A(\theta_{0},(x_{1},y_{1})); encode y2y_{2} using the updated model’s prediction pθ1​(y∣x2)p_{\theta_{1}}(y\mid x_{2}), incurring codelength ℓ​(θ1;x2,y2)\ell(\theta_{1};x_{2},y_{2}); and continue until all labels are encoded. The total codelength is
items.zh: 考虑在给定对应输入 x1,…,xn 的情况下编码标签序列 y1,…,yn。在序贯编码中，我们按如下步骤进行：使用初始模型的预测 pθ0(y|x1) 编码 y1，产生编码长度 ℓ(θ0; x1, y1)；将参数更新为 θ1 = A(θ0, (x1, y1))；使用更新后模型的预测 pθ1(y|x2) 编码 y2，产生编码长度 ℓ(θ1; x2, y2)；继续直到所有标签被编码。总编码长度为

items.en: A notable property of this scheme is that the decoder can exactly replicate the encoder’s model at each step, since both parties perform identical updates on exact copies of the same model. No separate transmission of model parameters is required—the model is implicitly communicated through the sequential encoding process. This provides an operational definition of the information content of a dataset relative to a learning algorithm: the number of bits required to transmit the labels using the model’s evolving predictions.
items.zh: 该方案的一个显著特性是，解码器可以在每一步精确复制编码器的模型，因为双方对模型的精确副本执行相同的更新。不需要单独传输模型参数——模型通过顺序编码过程隐式传达。这提供了数据集相对于学习算法的信息内容的操作定义：使用模型演化的预测传输标签所需的比特数。

items.en: The Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle, introduced by Rissanen (1978), operationalizes the parsimony principle for model selection, formalizing the intuition that the best model is one that most compresses the data. In its two-part form, the description length of data DD using model MM from a hypothesis class ℳ\mathcal{M} is
items.zh: 最小描述长度（MDL）原则由 Rissanen (1978) 引入，将模型选择的简约原则操作化，形式化了最佳模型是能最压缩数据的模型的直觉。在其两部分形式中，使用来自假设类 ℳ 的模型 M 对数据 D 的描述长度为

items.en: where L​(M)L(M) is the codelength required to describe the model and L​(D∣M)L(D\mid M) is the codelength of the data given the model. The MDL principle selects the model minimizing total description length, L​(D;M)L(D;M).
items.zh: 其中 L(M) 是描述模型所需的编码长度，L(D|M) 是给定模型下数据的编码长度。MDL 原则选择使总描述长度 L(D;M) 最小的模型。

items.en: Prequential coding provides an alternative formulation that avoids explicitly coding the model. The prequential codelength depends on the learning algorithm but not on any model complexity penalty, since the model is never directly transmitted. This makes prequential MDL particularly suitable for analyzing neural network fine-tuning, where the relationship between parameter count and effective complexity is unclear.
items.zh: 序贯编码提供了一种避免显式编码模型的替代公式。序贯编码长度取决于学习算法，而不依赖于任何模型复杂度惩罚，因为模型从未直接传输。这使得序贯 MDL 特别适用于分析神经网络微调，其中参数数量与有效复杂度之间的关系尚不明确。

items.en: Surplus description length (SDL), introduced by Whitney et al. (2021), quantifies the excess codelength incurred during learning relative to the optimal achievable loss. For a dataset DD and algorithm AA producing final parameters θ∗\theta^{*}, the SDL is defined as
items.zh: 剩余描述长度（SDL）由 Whitney 等人（2021）提出，用于量化学习过程中相对于最优可实现损失的额外编码长度。对于数据集 D 和产生最终参数 θ* 的算法 A，SDL 定义为

items.en: where L∗=infθL​(θ)L^{*}=\inf_{\theta}L(\theta) is the optimal expected loss achievable within the model class.
items.zh: 其中 L* = inf_θ L(θ) 是模型类内可实现的最优期望损失。

items.en: SDL captures the information cost of learning: additional bits spent during training beyond what the optimal model would require. It provides a measure of how quickly or efficiently an algorithm converges to optimal predictions. However, SDL requires access to an (effectively) infinite distribution of data to sample training examples from, which may be impractical and does not reflect data constraints common in fine-tuning settings. SDL also requires knowledge of L∗L^{*}, which may be unknown or uncomputable in practice. EDL addresses these points by replacing the optimal loss with the achieved test loss, yielding a fully computable measure that remains valid for any dataset size.
items.zh: SDL 捕捉了学习的信息成本：训练过程中超出最优模型所需的多余比特。它提供了算法收敛到最优预测的速度或效率的度量。然而，SDL 需要访问（有效的）无限数据分布以采样训练样本，这可能不切实际，且无法反映微调场景中常见的数据约束。SDL 还需要知道 L*，这在实践中可能未知或不可计算。EDL 通过用实际测试损失替代最优损失来解决这些问题，提供了一个完全可计算的度量，且对任何数据集大小均有效。

items.en: We now present formal definitions of prequential MDL and excess description length (EDL). These definitions make explicit the dependence on the training algorithm and provide clear operational implementations: both quantities can be computed directly from training logs and finite data without access to any oracle or asymptotic limit.
items.zh: 我们现在给出序贯 MDL 和额外描述长度（EDL）的形式化定义。这些定义明确了对训练算法的依赖，并提供了清晰的操作实现：两个量都可以直接从训练日志和有限数据中计算，无需访问任何预言或渐近极限。

items.en: Let D={(xi,yi)}i=1nD=\{(x_{i},y_{i})\}_{i=1}^{n} be a training dataset, θ0\theta_{0} initial model parameters, and AA a training algorithm. The prequential minimum description length is
items.zh: 令 D = {(x_i, y_i)}_{i=1}^n 为训练数据集，θ_0 为初始模型参数，A 为训练算法。序贯最小描述长度为

items.en: where θi=A​(θi−1,(xi,yi))\theta_{i}=A(\theta_{i-1},(x_{i},y_{i})) for i≥1i\geq 1.
items.zh: 其中对于 i ≥ 1，θ_i = A(θ_{i-1}, (x_i, y_i))。

items.en: The prequential MDL measures the total codelength required to encode the training labels using the evolving model as the coding distribution. Each term ℓ​(θi−1;xi,yi)\ell(\theta_{i-1};x_{i},y_{i}) represents the bits needed to encode label yiy_{i} using the model’s prediction before updating on example ii. The sum accumulates these costs across the entire dataset.
items.zh: 序贯 MDL 衡量使用演化模型作为编码分布来编码训练标签所需的总编码长度。每一项 ℓ(θ_{i-1}; x_i, y_i) 表示在更新第 i 个样本之前，使用模型预测编码标签 y_i 所需的比特数。总和累积了整个数据集上的这些成本。

items.en: In practice, training proceeds by batches rather than single examples. The definition extends naturally: for batch BjB_{j} processed at step jj, we accumulate ∑(x,y)∈Bjℓ​(θj−1;x,y)\sum_{(x,y)\in B_{j}}\ell(\theta_{j-1};x,y) before the update, where θj−1\theta_{j-1} denotes parameters before processing batch BjB_{j}. The order of summation within a batch does not affect the total, and the prequential interpretation remains valid since all examples in the batch are encoded using the pre-update parameters.
items.zh: 在实践中，训练是按批次而非单个样本进行的。该定义自然扩展：对于在第 j 步处理的批次 B_j，我们在更新前累积 ∑_{(x,y)∈B_j} ℓ(θ_{j-1}; x, y)，其中 θ_{j-1} 表示处理批次 B_j 之前的参数。批次内的求和顺序不影响总和，且序贯解释仍然有效，因为批次中的所有样本都使用更新前的参数进行编码。

items.en: The prequential MDL depends on the order in which examples are presented. For randomly shuffled data, this dependence introduces variance but does not change the expected value under the data distribution. When comparing MDL across experiments, the same ordering (or averaging over orderings) should be used.
items.zh: 序贯 MDL 依赖于样本呈现的顺序。对于随机打乱的数据，这种依赖引入了方差，但不会改变数据分布下的期望值。在比较不同实验的 MDL 时，应使用相同的顺序（或对顺序进行平均）。

items.en: Training typically continues beyond a single pass through the data, with multiple epochs refining the model’s parameters. Let θ∗\theta^{*} denote the final parameters after training completes, whether by convergence, early stopping, or a fixed compute budget. The test loss of the final model is
items.zh: 训练通常持续超过一轮数据遍历，多个 epoch 会优化模型参数。令 θ* 表示训练完成后的最终参数，无论是通过收敛、早停还是固定的算力预算。最终模型的测试损失为

items.en: estimated in practice by averaging over a held-out test set drawn from the same distribution as the training data.
items.zh: 在实践中，通过在从与训练数据相同分布中抽取的保留测试集上取平均来估计。

items.en: The EDL measures the gap between two quantities: the bits required to encode training labels using the evolving model during first exposure (MDL), and the bits that would be required to encode nn new samples from the same distribution using the final model (n⋅Ltest​(θ∗)n\cdot L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})). This gap represents the predictive information that has been absorbed into the model’s parameters—structure extracted from the training data that now enables more efficient encoding of test data.
items.zh: EDL 衡量两个量之间的差距：在首次暴露期间使用演化模型编码训练标签所需的比特数（MDL），以及使用最终模型编码来自同一分布的 n 个新样本所需的比特数（n·L_test(θ*)）。这个差距代表了已吸收到模型参数中的预测信息——从训练数据中提取的结构，现在使得测试数据的编码更高效。

items.en: To see this interpretation more clearly, consider the following decomposition. The MDL can be written as
items.zh: 为了更清晰地理解这一解释，考虑以下分解。MDL 可以写为

items.en: The first term sums the “excess” loss at each step—how much worse the current model is than the final model—while the second term is the residual codelength using the final model. Rearranging, we have
items.zh: 第一项求和了每一步的“额外”损失——当前模型比最终模型差多少——而第二项是使用最终模型的剩余编码长度。重新排列后，我们有

items.en: which makes explicit that EDL accumulates the per-example excess over the training trajectory.
items.zh: 这明确表明 EDL 累积了训练轨迹上每个样本的额外损失。

items.en: The prequential coding framework admits a compelling interpretation in terms of communication between two parties. Suppose Alice has trained a model and wishes to communicate the results of her training to Bob. They start with identical copies of the initial model θ0\theta_{0} and have agreed on the training algorithm AA, including all hyperparameters necessary to replicate the model and training process. Alice has the full training dataset DD; Bob has only the inputs {xi}i=1n\{x_{i}\}_{i=1}^{n}.
items.zh: 序贯编码框架允许在通信双方之间进行引人注目的解释。假设 Alice 训练了一个模型，并希望将训练结果传达给 Bob。他们从相同的初始模型 θ_0 副本开始，并已就训练算法 A（包括复制模型和训练过程所需的所有超参数）达成一致。Alice 拥有完整的训练数据集 D；Bob 只有输入 {x_i}_{i=1}^n。

items.en: Rather than transmitting the trained parameters directly, which may require billions of bits for large models, Alice can transmit the training labels, from which Bob can reconstruct an identical trained model. The protocol proceeds as follows. For each example ii, Alice encodes yiy_{i} using their shared current model’s predictive distribution pθi−1​(y∣xi)p_{\theta_{i-1}}(y\mid x_{i}) and transmits this encoding to Bob. Bob decodes yiy_{i} using the same distribution. Both parties then update their parameters identically: θi=A​(θi−1,(xi,yi))\theta_{i}=A(\theta_{i-1},(x_{i},y_{i})). After processing all examples, both have identical final parameters θ∗\theta^{*}.
items.zh: 与其直接传输训练好的参数（对于大模型可能需要数十亿比特），Alice 可以传输训练标签，Bob 可以从这些标签重建相同的训练模型。协议如下进行。对于每个样本 i，Alice 使用他们共享的当前模型的预测分布 p_{θ_{i-1}}(y|x_i) 对 y_i 进行编码，并将此编码传输给 Bob。Bob 使用相同的分布解码 y_i。然后双方都相同地更新其参数：θ_i = A(θ_{i-1}, (x_i, y_i))。处理完所有样本后，双方都有相同的最终参数 θ*。

items.en: The total communication cost is precisely the prequential MDL. This cost reflects the information in the training labels as measured by the model’s evolving ability to predict them. Labels that the model already predicts well require few bits; labels that surprise the model require many bits.
items.zh: 总通信成本正是序贯 MDL。这个成本反映了训练标签中的信息，以模型不断演化的预测能力来衡量。模型已经很好预测的标签需要很少的比特；让模型惊讶的标签需要很多比特。

items.en: The test loss Ltest​(θ∗)L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*}) measures how many bits per example the final model would still require to encode new samples from the distribution. If Bob, after receiving Alice’s transmission, encounters new examples from 𝒟\mathcal{D}, he expects to need Ltest​(θ∗)L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*}) bits per example to encode their labels. The EDL thus measures the difference between what Alice transmitted and what remains unexplained by the final model—the information Alice sent that has been “absorbed” (about 𝒟\mathcal{D}) into the model rather than remaining as residual encoding cost.
items.zh: 测试损失 $L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})$ 衡量最终模型在编码来自分布的新样本时，每个示例仍需要多少比特。如果 Bob 在收到 Alice 的传输后遇到来自 $\mathcal{D}$ 的新示例，他预计需要 $L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})$ 比特/示例来编码其标签。因此，EDL 衡量 Alice 传输的内容与最终模型未解释的内容之间的差异——即 Alice 发送的、已被模型“吸收”（关于 $\mathcal{D}$）的信息，而不是作为剩余编码成本的信息。

items.en: For comparison across datasets of different sizes or models with different parameter counts, we employ several normalizations of EDL.
items.zh: 为了在不同大小的数据集或不同参数数量的模型之间进行比较，我们采用了 EDL 的几种归一化方法。

items.en: The per-example EDL is EDLex=EDL/n\text{EDL}_{\text{ex}}=\text{EDL}/n, measuring average absorbed information per training example. This normalization is useful for comparing learning efficiency across dataset sizes.
items.zh: 每个示例的 EDL 为 $\text{EDL}_{\text{ex}}=\text{EDL}/n$，衡量每个训练示例平均吸收的信息。这种归一化有助于比较不同数据集大小下的学习效率。

items.en: The per-token EDL is EDLtok=EDL/D\text{EDL}_{\text{tok}}=\text{EDL}/D, where DD is the total number of tokens in the dataset scored in computing MDL (typically, the label tokens across all examples). For language modeling tasks where examples vary in length, this normalization accounts for the total volume of prediction.
items.zh: 每个 token 的 EDL 为 $\text{EDL}_{\text{tok}}=\text{EDL}/D$，其中 $D$ 是计算 MDL 时评分的数据集中的总 token 数（通常是所有示例的标签 token）。对于示例长度不同的语言建模任务，这种归一化考虑了预测的总量。

items.en: The per-parameter EDL is EDLpar=EDL/P\text{EDL}_{\text{par}}=\text{EDL}/P, where PP is the number of trainable parameters. This normalization is particularly relevant for parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods like LoRA, where only a subset of parameters are updated. It measures the information (bits) compressed per trainable parameter, providing insight into whether the parameter budget constrains the information that can be absorbed.
items.zh: 每个参数的 EDL 为 $\text{EDL}_{\text{par}}=\text{EDL}/P$，其中 $P$ 是可训练参数的数量。这种归一化对于 LoRA 等参数高效微调方法尤其相关，因为只有一部分参数被更新。它衡量每个可训练参数压缩的信息（比特），从而了解参数预算是否限制了可吸收的信息。

full.secZh: 3.5 与剩余描述长度的关系

items.en: EDL is closely related to the surplus description length of Whitney et al. (2021), differing primarily in the reference point used. SDL subtracts n⋅L∗n\cdot L^{*}, where L∗L^{*} is the optimal achievable loss; EDL subtracts n⋅Ltest​(θ∗)n\cdot L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*}), the achieved test loss.
items.zh: EDL 与 Whitney 等人 (2021) 的剩余描述长度密切相关，主要区别在于参考点。SDL 减去 $n\cdot L^{*}$，其中 $L^{*}$ 是最优可达损失；EDL 减去 $n\cdot L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})$，即达到的测试损失。

items.en: When the training algorithm successfully finds near-optimal parameters—that is, when Ltest​(θ∗)≈L∗L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})\approx L^{*}—the two quantities coincide. In general, EDL satisfies
items.zh: 当训练算法成功找到接近最优的参数时——即 $L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})\approx L^{*}$——这两个量一致。一般情况下，EDL 满足

items.en: The additional term n⋅(L∗−Ltest​(θ∗))n\cdot(L^{*}-L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})) is non-positive, reflecting that achieved test loss is at least as large as optimal loss. Thus EDL≤SDL\text{EDL}\leq\text{SDL}, with equality when the learner achieves the optimum.
items.zh: 附加项 $n\cdot(L^{*}-L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*}))$ 是非正的，反映了达到的测试损失至少与最优损失一样大。因此 $\text{EDL}\leq\text{SDL}$，当学习器达到最优时取等号。

items.en: The practical advantage of EDL over SDL is computability. Computing SDL requires knowing L∗L^{*}, which may be unavailable, especially for complex distributions or model classes where the optimal parameters are unknown. EDL requires only quantities that are directly measurable: the accumulated training loss during the first epoch and the test loss of the final model.
items.zh: EDL 相对于 SDL 的实际优势在于可计算性。计算 SDL 需要知道 $L^{*}$，这可能无法获得，尤其是对于复杂分布或模型类，其中最优参数未知。EDL 只需要直接可测量的量：第一个 epoch 期间的累积训练损失和最终模型的测试损失。

items.en: This section establishes fundamental properties of EDL, including non-negativity, relationships to online learning regret, and asymptotic behavior. We demonstrate that EDL satisfies the properties of operationally derived information-theoretic measures and is a valid description length. These results formalize the intuition that EDL measures absorbed generalizable information and provide foundations for interpreting empirical measurements.
items.zh: 本节建立了 EDL 的基本性质，包括非负性、与在线学习遗憾的关系以及渐近行为。我们证明 EDL 满足操作导出的信息论度量的性质，并且是一个有效的描述长度。这些结果形式化了 EDL 衡量吸收的可泛化信息的直觉，并为解释经验测量提供了基础。

items.en: A basic verification for any measure of “information absorbed” is that it should be non-negative: training should not destroy predictive information, on average. We establish this property under standard assumptions.
items.zh: 对任何“吸收信息”度量的基本验证是它应该是非负的：训练平均而言不应破坏预测信息。我们在标准假设下建立了这一性质。

items.en: A training algorithm AA is population-monotonic with respect to distribution 𝒟\mathcal{D} if, for all steps jj in the training trajectory,
items.zh: 如果对于训练轨迹中的所有步骤 $j$，训练算法 $A$ 关于分布 $\mathcal{D}$ 是总体单调的，则

items.en: where L​(θ)=𝔼(x,y)∼𝒟​[ℓ​(θ;x,y)]L(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_{(x,y)\sim\mathcal{D}}[\ell(\theta;x,y)] is the population loss. That is, each update does not increase the expected population loss in expectation over the randomness in the update (e.g., batch sampling).
items.zh: 其中 $L(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_{(x,y)\sim\mathcal{D}}[\ell(\theta;x,y)]$ 是总体损失。也就是说，每次更新在更新随机性（例如，批次采样）的期望下不会增加期望的总体损失。

items.en: Gradient descent on convex losses with appropriate learning rate
items.zh: 凸损失上具有适当学习率的梯度下降

items.en: SGD with sufficiently small learning rate on smooth losses
items.zh: 光滑损失上学习率足够小的 SGD

items.en: Any algorithm where early stopping prevents overfitting
items.zh: 任何提前停止防止过拟合的算法

items.en: Large learning rates that cause oscillation
items.zh: 导致振荡的大学习率

items.en: Training well past the optimal early stopping point on finite data
items.zh: 在有限数据上训练远超最优提前停止点

items.en: Algorithms that memorize training data at the expense of generalization
items.zh: 以牺牲泛化为代价记忆训练数据的算法

items.en: Let DD be drawn i.i.d. from distribution 𝒟\mathcal{D}, let the test loss be evaluated on an independent sample DtestD_{\text{test}} from 𝒟\mathcal{D}, and let AA be a population-monotonic algorithm (subsection 4.1). Then
items.zh: 设 $D$ 从分布 $\mathcal{D}$ 中独立同分布抽取，测试损失在来自 $\mathcal{D}$ 的独立样本 $D_{\text{test}}$ 上评估，且 $A$ 是总体单调算法（子节 4.1）。则

items.en: For algorithms that are not population-monotonic, EDL may be negative in expectation. Negative expected EDL indicates that the training procedure has degraded generalization—the model has “unlearned” predictive structure. This can occur when training far past the early stopping point or with pathological hyperparameters. In practice, we observe 𝔼​[EDL]≥0\mathbb{E}[\text{EDL}]\geq 0 for well-tuned training procedures (see companion empirical paper).
items.zh: 对于非群体单调的算法，EDL 的期望可能为负。负的期望 EDL 表明训练过程降低了泛化能力——模型“遗忘”了预测结构。当训练远超早停点或使用病态超参数时，可能发生这种情况。在实践中，对于调优良好的训练过程，我们观察到 𝔼[EDL] ≥ 0（参见配套实证论文）。

items.en: The expected test loss of the final model cannot exceed the expected test loss of the initial model, since training on data from 𝒟\mathcal{D} should not systematically worsen predictions on 𝒟\mathcal{D} for a population-monotonic algorithm. More formally, by the online-to-batch conversion, the expected loss of the final model is at most the average expected loss across the training trajectory.
items.zh: 最终模型的期望测试损失不能超过初始模型的期望测试损失，因为对于群体单调算法，在来自 𝒟 的数据上训练不应系统性地恶化对 𝒟 的预测。更正式地，通过在线到批量转换，最终模型的期望损失至多为训练轨迹上的平均期望损失。

items.en: where the second equality uses the i.i.d. assumption: conditioning on θi−1\theta_{i-1} (which depends on x1,y1,…,xi−1,yi−1x_{1},y_{1},\ldots,x_{i-1},y_{i-1}), the expectation of ℓ​(θi−1;xi,yi)\ell(\theta_{i-1};x_{i},y_{i}) over the fresh draw (xi,yi)∼𝒟(x_{i},y_{i})\sim\mathcal{D} equals L​(θi−1)L(\theta_{i-1}).
items.zh: 其中第二个等式使用了 i.i.d. 假设：在给定 θ_{i-1}（依赖于 x_1, y_1, …, x_{i-1}, y_{i-1}）的条件下，ℓ(θ_{i-1}; x_i, y_i) 在新抽取 (x_i, y_i) ∼ 𝒟 上的期望等于 L(θ_{i-1})。

items.en: For the test loss, 𝔼​[Ltest​(θ∗)]=L​(θ∗)\mathbb{E}[L_{\text{test}}(\theta^{*})]=L(\theta^{*}) by independence of test data.
items.zh: 对于测试损失，由于测试数据的独立性，𝔼[L_test(θ*)] = L(θ*)。

items.en: Each term L​(θi−1)−L​(θ∗)L(\theta_{i-1})-L(\theta^{*}) is the expected excess loss of the intermediate model relative to the final model. Since θ∗\theta^{*} is obtained by training on the full dataset (or beyond), and training generally improves expected loss, we have L​(θi−1)≥L​(θ∗)L(\theta_{i-1})\geq L(\theta^{*}) for all ii. Thus each term is non-negative, and so is their sum. ∎
items.zh: 每一项 L(θ_{i-1}) - L(θ*) 是中间模型相对于最终模型的期望超额损失。由于 θ* 是通过在完整数据集（或更多）上训练得到的，且训练通常会改善期望损失，因此对所有 i 有 L(θ_{i-1}) ≥ L(θ*)。因此每一项非负，其和也非负。∎

items.en: For finite test sets, the estimated test loss has variance, and individual realizations of EDL may be slightly negative. This sampling noise vanishes as test set size increases.
items.zh: 对于有限的测试集，估计的测试损失存在方差，EDL 的个别实现可能略微为负。这种采样噪声随着测试集大小的增加而消失。

items.en: Online learning theory provides bounds on cumulative loss through the notion of regret. The regret of an algorithm relative to a fixed comparator θ\theta is
items.zh: 在线学习理论通过遗憾的概念提供了累积损失的界。算法相对于固定比较器 θ 的遗憾为

items.en: This measures how much worse the online algorithm performs compared to having used θ\theta, the best model from the hypothesis class for each example, throughout.
items.zh: 这衡量了在线算法相对于始终使用 θ（假设类中每个样本的最佳模型）的性能差多少。

items.en: EDL differs from regret in that regret quantifies the cumulative excess train set loss relative to that of the fixed, globally optimal predictor of the specific sequence of train data, agnostic to arbitrary redundancy in examples, whereas EDL quantifies the excess minimum expected codelength required to encode all unique examples in the train set using the online-trained model rather than a fixed predictor with constant generalization error.
items.zh: EDL 与遗憾的不同之处在于，遗憾量化了相对于固定全局最优预测器（针对特定训练数据序列，不考虑样本中的任意冗余）的累积超额训练集损失，而 EDL 量化了使用在线训练模型而非具有恒定泛化误差的固定预测器来编码训练集中所有唯一样本所需的超额最小期望码长。

items.en: Regret measures the additional codelength incurred through online learning because the best model a posteriori for encoding each example was not used. EDL quantifies the additional codelength incurred through learning because the initial model did not generalize as well as could have. Regret quantifies excess codelength relative to an optimal, fixed predictor of the train set data, whereas EDL quantifies excess codelength relative to a fixed predictor which generalizes over the test distribution with error equal to the test loss. EDL, regret, and SDL all converge to the same value in the asymptotic limit.
items.zh: 遗憾衡量了由于未使用后验最优模型编码每个样本而在线学习产生的额外码长。EDL 量化了由于初始模型未能像可能的那样泛化而学习产生的额外码长。遗憾量化了相对于训练集数据的最优固定预测器的超额码长，而 EDL 量化了相对于一个固定预测器（其在测试分布上的泛化误差等于测试损失）的超额码长。在渐近极限下，EDL、遗憾和 SDL 都收敛到相同的值。

items.en: This decomposition expresses MDL as the loss of a fixed model plus the regret incurred by the learning algorithm. Taking θ=θ∗\theta=\theta^{*} (the final trained parameters) and noting that ∑i=1nℓ​(θ∗;xi,yi)≈n⋅Ltrain​(θ∗)\sum_{i=1}^{n}\ell(\theta^{*};x_{i},y_{i})\approx n\cdot L_{\text{train}}(\theta^{*}) where LtrainL_{\text{train}} is the training loss, we obtain
items.zh: 该分解将 MDL 表示为固定模型的损失加上学习算法产生的遗憾。取 θ = θ*（最终训练参数），并注意到 ∑_{i=1}^n ℓ(θ*; x_i, y_i) ≈ n · L_train(θ*)，其中 L_train 是训练损失，我们得到

items.en: Standard online learning algorithms achieve regret bounds of O​(n)O(\sqrt{n}) for convex losses, implying that MDL/n→Ltrain​(θ∗)\text{MDL}/n\to L_{\text{train}}(\theta^{*}) as n→∞n\to\infty. The prequential codelength per example converges to the per-example training loss of the final model.
items.zh: 标准在线学习算法对于凸损失可实现 O(√n) 的遗憾界，这意味着当 n → ∞ 时，MDL/n → L_train(θ*)。每个样本的预序码长收敛到最终模型的每个样本训练损失。

items.en: If the algorithm achieves sublinear regret Rn​(θ∗)=o​(n)R_{n}(\theta^{*})=o(n), then
items.zh: 如果算法实现次线性遗憾 R_n(θ*) = o(n)，则

items.en: as n→∞n\to\infty. When the model generalizes perfectly (Ltrain=LtestL_{\mathrm{train}}=L_{\mathrm{test}}), the per-example EDL vanishes asymptotically.
items.zh: 当 n → ∞ 时。当模型完美泛化（L_train = L_test）时，每个样本的 EDL 渐近消失。

items.en: This corollary connects EDL to the generalization gap. Non-zero per-example EDL in the asymptotic regime indicates a gap between training and test performance due to overfitting or distribution shift.
items.zh: 该推论将 EDL 与泛化差距联系起来。渐近状态下非零的每个样本 EDL 表明由于过拟合或分布偏移，训练和测试性能之间存在差距。

items.en: As the asymptotic regime is characterized by i.i.d. sampling n→∞n\rightarrow\infty examples from an (effectively) infinite distribution, non-zero per-example EDL indicates redundancy in train or test examples, spurious correlations within the set of training examples (which should vanish in expectation as n→∞n\rightarrow\infty), and/or a distribution shift between the train and test distributions. Large, negative per-example EDL in the asymptotic regime—while not a valid codelength—does have practical value, revealing that the model has absorbed information specific to the training set that does not transfer to test data.
items.zh: 由于渐近状态的特征是从（有效）无限分布中 i.i.d. 采样 n → ∞ 个样本，非零的每个样本 EDL 表明训练或测试样本中的冗余、训练样本集内的虚假相关性（当 n → ∞ 时应在期望中消失），和/或训练与测试分布之间的分布偏移。渐近状态下大的负每个样本 EDL——虽然不是有效的码长——具有实际价值，揭示了模型吸收了训练集特有的信息，而这些信息不能迁移到测试数据。

items.en: As dataset size grows, EDL converges to surplus description length under conditions ensuring that the trained model approaches optimality.
items.zh: 随着数据集规模增长，在确保训练模型接近最优的条件下，EDL 收敛到剩余描述长度。

items.en: Suppose the learning algorithm is consistent for the model class Θ\Theta under distribution 𝒟\mathcal{D}: L​(θ∗)→L∗L(\theta^{*})\to L^{*} almost surely as n→∞n\to\infty, where L∗=infθ∈ΘL​(θ)L^{*}=\inf_{\theta\in\Theta}L(\theta). Then
items.zh: 假设学习算法对于模型类 Θ 在分布 𝒟 下是一致的：当 n → ∞ 时，L(θ*) → L* 几乎必然，其中 L* = inf_{θ∈Θ} L(θ)。则

items.en: and under consistency, this difference grows sublinearly in nn.
items.zh: 并且在一致性下，该差值随 n 次线性增长。

items.en: The rate of convergence depends on the model class:
items.zh: 收敛速度取决于模型类：

items.en: For finite hypothesis classes: O​(log⁡|Θ|n)O\left(\sqrt{\frac{\log|\Theta|}{n}}\right)
items.zh: 对于有限假设类：O(log|Θ|/n)

items.en: For parametric models with bounded complexity: O​(1n)O\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\right)
items.zh: 对于复杂度有界的参数模型：O(1/n)

items.en: For overparameterized neural networks: rates depend on implicit regularization and may not follow classical scaling
items.zh: 对于过参数化神经网络：速率取决于隐式正则化，可能不遵循经典缩放

items.en: We do not claim rates for neural networks. The theorem establishes qualitative convergence under the consistency assumption, which can be verified emprically for specific architectures and training procedures.
items.zh: 我们不声称神经网络的速率。该定理在一致性假设下建立了定性收敛，该假设可通过特定架构和训练过程进行经验验证。

items.en: Under consistency and given suitable rates of convergence, the gap between achieved and optimal loss vanishes, and EDL coincides with SDL. This provides theoretical grounding for using EDL as a practical, finite-data analog to the information-theoretically motivated SDL.
items.zh: 在一致性及合适的收敛速率下，实现损失与最优损失之间的差距消失，EDL 与 SDL 一致。这为使用 EDL 作为信息论驱动的 SDL 的实用有限数据模拟提供了理论基础。

full.sec: 4.4 Bound on Expected Generalization Error Improvement
full.secZh: 4.4 期望泛化误差改进的界限

items.en: EDL provides an upper bound on how much the expected generalization error (loss) improves through training.
items.zh: EDL 提供了期望泛化误差（损失）通过训练改善程度的上界。

items.en: For a population-monotonic algorithm, the expected improvement in population loss decomposes as:
items.zh: 对于群体单调算法，群体损失的期望改善可分解为：

items.en: where L¯=1n​∑i=1n𝔼​[L​(θi−1)]\bar{L}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n}\mathbb{E}\left[L(\theta_{i-1})\right] is the average expected population loss across the training trajectory.
items.zh: 其中 L̄ = (1/n)∑_{i=1}^n E[L(θ_{i-1})] 是训练轨迹上平均期望群体损失。

items.en: The total improvement L​(θ0)−𝔼​[L​(θ∗)]L(\theta_{0})-\mathbb{E}\left[L(\theta^{*})\right] can be decomposed into two parts:
items.zh: 总改善 L(θ₀) - E[L(θ*)] 可分解为两部分：

items.en: L​(θ0)−L¯L(\theta_{0})-\bar{L}: improvement during the training trajectory (always non-negative for population-monotonic algorithms)
items.zh: L(θ₀) - L̄：训练轨迹期间的改善（对于群体单调算法始终非负）

items.en: 𝔼​[EDL]/n\mathbb{E}[\text{EDL}]/n: per-example absorbed generalizable information, measuring how much better the final model performs on the population distribution compared to the trajectory average
items.zh: E[EDL]/n：每个样本吸收的可泛化信息，衡量最终模型在群体分布上的表现相比轨迹平均值的提升程度

items.en: The result follows from L​(θ0)−L​(θ∗)=(L​(θ0)−L¯)+(L¯−L​(θ∗))L(\theta_{0})-L(\theta^{*})=\left(L(\theta_{0})-\bar{L}\right)+\left(\bar{L}-L(\theta^{*})\right). ∎
items.zh: 该结果由 L(θ₀) - L(θ*) = (L(θ₀) - L̄) + (L̄ - L(θ*)) 得出。∎

items.en: This bound shows that EDL/n\text{EDL}/n upper bounds the improvement attributable to learning during training (the L¯−L​(θ∗)\bar{L}-L(\theta^{*}) term), while the remaining improvement (L​(θ0)−L¯)(L(\theta_{0})-\bar{L}) reflects loss reduction during early training before substantial learning occurs.
items.zh: 该界表明 EDL/n 上界了训练期间学习带来的改善（L̄ - L(θ*) 项），而剩余改善 (L(θ₀) - L̄) 反映了在大量学习发生之前的早期训练中的损失减少。

items.en: Computed prequentially, EDL inherently depends on the training algorithm and is not an intrinsic property of the model class and dataset alone.
items.zh: 按序贯方式计算，EDL 本质上依赖于训练算法，并非仅模型类和数据集的内在属性。

items.en: For fixed DD and θ0\theta_{0}, different algorithms AA and A′A^{\prime} can yield different EDL values: EDL​(D;θ0,A)≠EDL​(D;θ0,A′)\mathrm{EDL}(D;\theta_{0},A)\neq\mathrm{EDL}(D;\theta_{0},A^{\prime}), in general.
items.zh: 对于固定的 D 和 θ₀，不同的算法 A 和 A' 可产生不同的 EDL 值：通常 EDL(D; θ₀, A) ≠ EDL(D; θ₀, A')。

items.en: This dependence arises through both MDL (determined by the loss trajectory) and the final test loss (determined by where training terminates). A more aggressive learning rate might achieve lower test loss but incur higher MDL due to initially worse predictions; a conservative learning rate might have lower MDL but higher test loss.
items.zh: 这种依赖性通过 MDL（由损失轨迹决定）和最终测试损失（由训练终止位置决定）产生。更激进的学习率可能实现更低的测试损失，但由于初始预测更差而导致更高的 MDL；保守的学习率可能具有更低的 MDL 但更高的测试损失。

items.en: This algorithm dependence is not a defect but a feature: different training procedures genuinely differ in how efficiently they extract information from data. When comparing EDL across settings, the algorithm should be held fixed or explicitly varied as an experimental factor.
items.zh: 这种算法依赖性并非缺陷而是特性：不同的训练过程在从数据中提取信息的效率上确实存在差异。在跨设置比较 EDL 时，应固定算法或将其作为实验因素显式变化。

items.en: To build intuition for EDL and clarify common confusions about information in learning, we present a series of toy models. Each illustrates a specific phenomenon and provides insight into when EDL signatures indicate elicitation versus teaching.
items.zh: 为建立对 EDL 的直觉并澄清关于学习中信息的常见混淆，我们呈现一系列玩具模型。每个模型说明一种特定现象，并提供关于 EDL 信号何时指示引出与何时指示教学的见解。

items.en: The following toy models are intended as conceptual illustrations rather than rigorous characterizations of neural network learning. Each model captures one phenomenon in isolation, whereas real learning involves interactions among multiple effects. The functional forms (e.g., linear learning curves, coupon collector dynamics) are chosen for analytical tractability and should be viewed as approximations. The qualitative predictions that teaching and elicitation have different EDL signatures are validated empirically in a companion paper.
items.zh: 以下玩具模型旨在作为概念性说明，而非神经网络学习的严格刻画。每个模型孤立地捕捉一种现象，而真实学习涉及多种效应的相互作用。函数形式（如线性学习曲线、优惠券收集器动力学）为分析可处理性而选择，应视为近似。关于教学和引出具有不同 EDL 信号的定性预测在配套论文中通过经验验证。

full.sec: 5.1 Random Labels: EDL Equals Zero When Nothing Generalizes
full.secZh: 5.1 随机标签：无泛化时 EDL 为零

items.capEn: Figure 1: When labels are random, test loss LtestL_{\text{test}} remains constant since no generalizable pattern can be learned. MDL and the residual codelength n⋅Ltestn\cdot L_{\text{test}} both increase linearly (in expectation), proportional to the number of training examples nn. Since no learnable structure exists in the data, EDL=MDL−n⋅Ltest≈0\text{EDL}=\text{MDL}-n\cdot L_{\text{test}}\approx 0, and the amount of generalizable information absorbed is negligible.
items.capZh: 图 1：当标签随机时，测试损失 L_test 保持不变，因为没有可泛化的模式可学习。MDL 和剩余码长 n·L_test 均线性增加（期望上），与训练样本数 n 成正比。由于数据中不存在可学习结构，EDL = MDL - n·L_test ≈ 0，吸收的可泛化信息量可忽略。

items.en: Our first toy model establishes a crucial baseline: when training labels carry no predictive information about test labels, EDL should be zero. This validates that EDL measures learned structure of the data distribution rather than mere computational effort or overfitting.
items.zh: 我们的第一个玩具模型建立了一个关键基线：当训练标签不携带关于测试标签的预测信息时，EDL 应为零。这验证了 EDL 衡量的是数据分布中学习到的结构，而非单纯的计算努力或过拟合。

items.en: Consider a setting where labels are generated independently of inputs and independently between training and test sets. Specifically, let y∼Uniform​(𝒴)y\sim\text{Uniform}(\mathcal{Y}) independently for each example, with |𝒴|=k|\mathcal{Y}|=k. The training labels {yi}i=1n\{y_{i}\}_{i=1}^{n} and test labels are drawn from this same marginal distribution but with no shared structure—knowing the training labels provides no information about test labels.
items.zh: 考虑一个标签独立于输入生成、且训练集与测试集之间标签独立的设定。具体地，每个样本独立地服从 y∼Uniform​(𝒴)，其中 |𝒴|=k。训练标签 {yi}i=1n 和测试标签均来自相同的边缘分布，但不存在共享结构——知道训练标签对测试标签不提供任何信息。

items.en: In this setting, the optimal predictor is the uniform distribution regardless of training data, achieving loss log⁡k\log k per example. The initial model, if well-calibrated, begins near this optimum. Training on random labels may cause the model to memorize the training set, but this memorization does not reduce test loss.
items.zh: 在此设定下，无论训练数据如何，最优预测器都是均匀分布，每个样本的损失为 log​k。初始模型若校准良好，则接近此最优值。在随机标签上训练可能导致模型记忆训练集，但这种记忆不会降低测试损失。

items.en: The prequential MDL is determined by the model’s predictions during training. If the model maintains predictions near uniform (the rational response to recognizing random labels), MDL is approximately n​log⁡kn\log k. If the model instead memorizes training labels, later training examples may have higher loss before being memorized, but the eventual test loss remains at log⁡k\log k since memorization does not generalize.
items.zh: 序贯 MDL 由模型在训练期间的预测决定。若模型保持预测接近均匀（识别出随机标签后的理性反应），则 MDL 约为 n​log​k。若模型记忆训练标签，则后续训练样本在被记忆前可能具有更高损失，但最终测试损失仍为 log​k，因为记忆无法泛化。

items.en: In either case, EDL=MDL−n⋅Ltest≈n​log⁡k−n​log⁡k=0\text{EDL}=\text{MDL}-n\cdot L_{\text{test}}\approx n\log k-n\log k=0. The model cannot compress the test labels any better than chance, so no predictive information has been absorbed from training.
items.zh: 无论哪种情况，EDL=MDL−n⋅Ltest≈n​log​k−n​log​k=0。模型无法比随机更好地压缩测试标签，因此未从训练中吸收任何预测信息。

items.en: For i.i.d. random labels independent between train and test, 𝔼​[EDL]=0\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{EDL}]=0.
items.zh: 对于训练集与测试集之间独立同分布的随机标签，𝔼​[EDL]=0。

items.en: This result provides a simple and practical validation for empirical measurements: if EDL is substantially positive, there must be learnable structure in the data. Random labels yield EDL near zero regardless of the training effort or computational resources expended.
items.zh: 这一结果为经验测量提供了简单而实用的验证：若 EDL 显著为正，则数据中必然存在可学习的结构。随机标签产生的 EDL 接近零，无论训练努力或计算资源如何。

full.sec: 5.2 Hypothesis Collapse: One Label Can Eliminate Many Bits of Uncertainty
full.secZh: 5.2 假设坍缩：一个标签可消除许多比特的不确定性

items.en: A common confusion about information in learning is the belief that a single binary label can provide at most one bit of information. This is incorrect when the label resolves uncertainty about which rule governs the data.
items.zh: 关于学习中的信息，一个常见的困惑是认为单个二值标签最多只能提供一比特信息。当标签消除了关于数据规则的歧义时，这种观点是错误的。

items.en: Consider a learner with a hypothesis class ℋ={h1,…,hm}\mathcal{H}=\{h_{1},\ldots,h_{m}\} representing possible input-output relationships for data distribution 𝒟:𝒳×𝒴\mathcal{D}:\mathcal{X}\times\mathcal{Y}. The learner begins with a uniform prior over hypotheses. A single example (x,y)∈𝒳×𝒴(x,y)\in\mathcal{X}\times\mathcal{Y} is observed, and suppose this example is diagnostic: only one hypothesis hjh_{j} is consistent with the observed input-output pair, while all others predict different labels for input xx.
items.zh: 考虑一个学习器，其假设类 ℋ={h1,…,hm} 表示数据分布 𝒟:𝒳×𝒴 可能的输入-输出关系。学习器从假设上的均匀先验开始。观察到单个样本 (x,y)∈𝒳×𝒴，假设该样本具有诊断性：仅有一个假设 hj 与观测的输入-输出对一致，而其他所有假设对输入 x 预测不同的标签。

items.en: Before observing the example, the learner’s uncertainty about which hypothesis is correct is log⁡m\log m bits. After observing (x,y)(x,y), this uncertainty drops to zero, as the learner now knows hjh_{j} is the true hypothesis. The single example has provided log⁡m\log m bits of information about the underlying rule, even though the label yy itself has at most log⁡|𝒴|\log|\mathcal{Y}| bits of entropy.
items.zh: 在观察样本前，学习器关于哪个假设正确的不确定性为 log​m 比特。观察 (x,y) 后，不确定性降至零，因为学习器现在知道 hj 是真实假设。单个样本提供了关于底层规则的 log​m 比特信息，尽管标签 yy 本身的熵最多为 log​|𝒴| 比特。

items.en: This phenomenon explains how EDL can be large even when training on few examples. If those examples are highly diagnostic, meaning they eliminate many hypotheses about how to perform a task, each example contributes information proportional to the logarithm of the number of hypotheses eliminated, as opposed to the naive label entropy.
items.zh: 这一现象解释了为何即使训练样本很少，EDL 也可能很大。如果这些样本具有高度诊断性，即它们消除了许多关于如何执行任务的假设，每个样本贡献的信息与消除的假设数量的对数成正比，而非简单的标签熵。

items.en: Consider a Bayesian learner with hypothesis class ℋ={h1,…,hm}\mathcal{H}=\{h_{1},\ldots,h_{m}\} and uniform prior. Suppose:
items.zh: 考虑一个贝叶斯学习器，假设类 ℋ={h1,…,hm} 且先验均匀。假设：

items.en: The example (x,y)(x,y) is diagnostic, meaning that exactly one hypothesis hjh_{j} satisfies hj​(x)=yh_{j}(x)=y.
items.zh: 样本 (x,y) 具有诊断性，即恰好一个假设 hj 满足 hj​(x)=y。

items.en: The hypothesis are maximally distinguishing on xx: for each label y′∈𝒴y^{\prime}\in\mathcal{Y}, exactly m/|𝒴|m/|\mathcal{Y}| hypotheses predict y′y^{\prime} for input x.
items.zh: 假设在 xx 上最大程度区分：对于每个标签 y′∈𝒴，恰好有 m/|𝒴| 个假设对输入 x 预测 y′。

items.en: Then, a single example contributes exactly log⁡|𝒴|\log|\mathcal{Y}| bits to EDL, and the generalization improvement is log⁡m\log m bits, the entropy of the full hypothesis space.
items.zh: 那么，单个样本恰好贡献 log​|𝒴| 比特给 EDL，而泛化改进为 log​m 比特，即整个假设空间的熵。

items.en: More generally, without assumption (ii), a single example can contribute at most min​(log⁡|𝒴|,log⁡m)\mathrm{min}\left(\log|\mathcal{Y}|,\log m\right) bits to EDL, where the codelength contribution is determined by the label entropy under the predictive distribution, while the generalization improvement is determined by the hypothesis entropy reduction.
items.zh: 更一般地，若无假设（ii），单个样本最多贡献 min​(log​|𝒴|,log​m) 比特给 EDL，其中码长贡献由预测分布下的标签熵决定，而泛化改进由假设熵的减少决定。

items.en: Note that since EDL measures the amount of information present in the observed train data that the model has gained about the test distribution, the model can gain at most as much generalizable information as the train data contains (i.e., EDL = MDL), even if a single example collapses the entire hypothesis space, reducing its uncertainty to zero.
items.zh: 注意，由于 EDL 衡量的是模型从观测到的训练数据中获得的关于测试分布的信息量，即使单个样本坍缩了整个假设空间，将其不确定性降至零，模型最多也只能获得训练数据中包含的可泛化信息量（即 EDL = MDL）。

items.en: In the context of elicitation, this toy model suggests why minimal fine-tuning can dramatically improve performance. If a pretrained model “contains” a capability in the sense that the correct hypothesis is among a small set,222For example, correct responses to the question, “What is 2 + 2?” may be “4,” “A simple addition problem,” or “2×22\times 2.” Though the correct hypothesis will depend on the specifics of the task, the hypothesis space is limited, and a single example may suffice to remove the uncertainty about which to choose. one or few examples may suffice to identify which hypothesis to use. The model already has the capability; the examples simply disambiguate which of its latent competencies to apply.
items.zh: 在激发（elicitation）的背景下，这个玩具模型提示了为何极少的微调就能显著提升性能。如果预训练模型“包含”某种能力，即正确假设位于一个小集合中，那么一个或几个样本可能就足以消除不确定性，从而确定使用哪个假设。模型已具备该能力；样本只是消歧了应应用其潜在能力中的哪一个。

full.sec: 5.3 Disjoint Subdistributions: Expected Generalization Gain Scales with Coverage
full.secZh: 5.3 不相交子分布：期望泛化增益随覆盖度缩放

items.en: Learning a rule that applies only to a subset of the input distribution contributes proportionally less to expected generalization. This toy model formalizes the intuition that rare patterns matter less for overall performance.
items.zh: 学习一个仅适用于输入分布子集的规则，对期望泛化的贡献成比例地较小。这个玩具模型形式化了稀有模式对整体性能影响较小的直觉。

items.en: Let the data distribution 𝒟\mathcal{D} be a mixture of KK disjoint subdistributions:
items.zh: 设数据分布 𝒟 是 K 个不相交的子分布的混合：

items.en: where each 𝒟j\mathcal{D}_{j} has support 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j} with 𝒳j∩𝒳j′=∅\mathcal{X}_{j}\cap\mathcal{X}_{j^{\prime}}=\emptyset for j≠j′j\neq j^{\prime}, and ∑jπj=1\sum_{j}\pi_{j}=1. Suppose that within each subdistribution, there is a “rule” that, once learned, reduces the per-example loss by 1 bit (or some fixed amount Δ\Delta).
items.zh: 其中每个 𝒟j\mathcal{D}_{j} 的支撑集为 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j}，且对于 j≠j′j\neq j^{\prime} 有 𝒳j∩𝒳j′=∅\mathcal{X}_{j}\cap\mathcal{X}_{j^{\prime}}=\emptyset，并且 ∑jπj=1\sum_{j}\pi_{j}=1。假设在每个子分布内存在一条“规则”，一旦学习到该规则，每个示例的损失将减少 1 比特（或某个固定量 Δ\Delta）。

items.en: If the training data covers only subdistribution jj with mixture weight πj\pi_{j}, the model can learn the corresponding rule and reduce loss on inputs from 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j}. However, test data is drawn from the full mixture 𝒟\mathcal{D}. The expected improvement in test loss is only πj⋅Δ\pi_{j}\cdot\Delta, the loss reduction on 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j} weighted by the probability of encountering inputs from subdistribution jj.
items.zh: 如果训练数据仅覆盖混合权重为 πj\pi_{j} 的子分布 jj，模型可以学习相应的规则并降低来自 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j} 的输入上的损失。然而，测试数据来自完整混合分布 𝒟\mathcal{D}。测试损失的预期改进仅为 πj⋅Δ\pi_{j}\cdot\Delta，即 𝒳j\mathcal{X}_{j} 上的损失减少乘以遇到子分布 jj 输入的概率。

items.en: Learning a rule that reduces loss by Δ\Delta bits on a subdistribution with mixture weight π\pi contributes approximately π⋅Δ\pi\cdot\Delta bits per example to expected generalization improvement.
items.zh: 学习一条在混合权重为 π\pi 的子分布上将损失减少 Δ\Delta 比特的规则，对预期泛化改进的贡献约为每示例 π⋅Δ\pi\cdot\Delta 比特。

items.en: This model clarifies the relationship between total information absorbed (computed on training data), expected generalization (computed over the test distribution), and generalizable information absorbed (EDL, computed on training and test data). A training set concentrated on a rare subdistribution may yield high total information absorbed if the model learns to predict that subdistribution well, but the expected generalization gain over the entire test distribution, EDL, is small because the learned structure applies to only a small fraction of test inputs.
items.zh: 该模型阐明了总吸收信息（在训练数据上计算）、预期泛化（在测试分布上计算）和可泛化吸收信息（EDL，在训练和测试数据上计算）之间的关系。集中在稀有子分布上的训练集可能产生较高的总吸收信息（如果模型学会了很好地预测该子分布），但整个测试分布上的预期泛化增益（即 EDL）很小，因为学到的结构仅适用于一小部分测试输入。

items.en: In practice, this phenomenon affects the interpretation of EDL signatures. Low EDL does not necessarily indicate low practical improvement on all subsets of the test distribution if the training data overrepresents rare cases. Conversely, modest EDL concentrated on high-probability regions may translate to substantial practical gains if learning is rapid (such as is the case for elicitation).
items.zh: 在实践中，这一现象影响了对 EDL 特征的解读。如果训练数据过度代表稀有情况，低 EDL 并不一定意味着测试分布的所有子集上实际改进很小。相反，集中在高概率区域的适度 EDL 可能转化为显著的实际收益（如果学习速度很快，例如在 elicitation 的情况下）。

full.sec: 5.4 Coupon Collector Dynamics: Phase Transitions in Learning
full.secZh: 5.4 优惠券收集器动力学：学习中的相变

items.en: When a task requires exposure to multiple distinct concepts or pieces of knowledge before generalization is possible, marginal contributions to EDL (per train example or label token) can exhibit non-monotonic scaling with dataset size. This toy model illustrates how coverage requirements can result in phase transitions.
items.zh: 当一项任务需要接触多个不同的概念或知识片段才能实现泛化时，EDL 的边际贡献（每训练示例或标签 token）可能随数据集大小呈现非单调的缩放。这个简单模型说明了覆盖要求如何导致相变。

items.en: Consider a task with nn distinct concepts, where the model must see at least one example of each concept to generalize, which occurs at time TT (when all concepts have been encountered). This is analogous to the coupon collector’s problem: here, examples are drawn uniformly from concepts (coupons), and the model collects concepts until all nn are represented.
items.zh: 考虑一个包含 nn 个不同概念的任务，模型必须至少看到每个概念的一个示例才能泛化，这发生在时间 TT（当所有概念都被遇到时）。这类似于优惠券收集问题：这里，示例从概念（优惠券）中均匀抽取，模型收集概念直到所有 nn 个都被表示。

items.en: In the coupon collector’s problem, the expected number of draws to collect all nn coupons is 𝔼​[T]=n⋅Hn≈n​log⁡n\mathbb{E}[T]=n\cdot H_{n}\approx n\log n, where HnH_{n} is the nn-th harmonic number. Before achieving full coverage, the model cannot reliably predict examples from unseen concepts, and test loss remains high on those concepts.
items.zh: 在优惠券收集问题中，收集所有 nn 个优惠券所需的期望抽取次数为 𝔼​[T]=n⋅Hn≈n​log⁡n\mathbb{E}[T]=n\cdot H_{n}\approx n\log n，其中 HnH_{n} 是第 nn 个调和数。在达到完全覆盖之前，模型无法可靠地预测来自未见概念的例子，测试损失在这些概念上仍然很高。

items.en: The EDL trajectory shows three phases. Initially, when few concepts have been seen, MDL accumulates rapidly (each new example is surprising) but test loss remains high (many concepts are still unknown), yielding moderate EDL. As training progresses toward the coverage threshold, more concepts are learned, and both MDL accumulation slows (fewer novel concepts) and test loss drops (more concepts become predictable), causing EDL to increase. Beyond full coverage, additional examples provide diminishing information because they are redundant with known concepts, and EDL saturates or grows slowly.
items.zh: EDL 轨迹显示出三个阶段。最初，当只有少数概念被看到时，MDL 快速累积（每个新示例都是令人惊讶的），但测试损失仍然很高（许多概念仍然未知），导致 EDL 适中。随着训练向覆盖阈值推进，更多概念被学习，MDL 累积减慢（新概念减少）且测试损失下降（更多概念变得可预测），导致 EDL 增加。超过完全覆盖后，额外示例提供的信息递减，因为它们与已知概念冗余，EDL 饱和或缓慢增长。

items.en: For a task requiring coverage of nn concepts, EDL shows an accelerating phase as kk, the number of examples trained on so far, approaches n​log⁡nn\log n, corresponding to the transition from partial to full coverage.
items.zh: 对于需要覆盖 nn 个概念的任务，当已训练的示例数量 kk 接近 n​log⁡nn\log n 时，EDL 呈现加速阶段，对应于从部分覆盖到完全覆盖的转变。

items.en: This model is relevant for teaching scenarios where the capability requires learning multiple components. The EDL signature of teaching—a phase where per-example EDL (significantly) increases—reflects the coverage dynamics. Until sufficient concepts are observed, the model cannot fully generalize, and additional examples provide increasing marginal value.
items.zh: 该模型适用于需要学习多个组件的能力的教学场景。教学中的 EDL 特征——每示例 EDL（显著）增加的阶段——反映了覆盖动态。在观察到足够的概念之前，模型无法完全泛化，额外示例提供递增的边际价值。

items.capEn: Figure 2: In the low-coverage regime (Phase 1, green), additional examples provide opportunities to learn generalizable patterns from relatively few known concepts. The rate of information absorption peaks as full coverage of concepts is approached (Phase 2, yellow). Beyond the coverage threshold (peak), learning enters the full-coverage regime (Phase 3, red), as additional examples yield diminishing predictive information that can be absorbed.
items.capZh: 图 2：在低覆盖区域（阶段 1，绿色），额外示例提供了从相对较少的已知概念中学习可泛化模式的机会。当接近概念的完全覆盖时（阶段 2，黄色），信息吸收率达到峰值。超过覆盖阈值（峰值）后，学习进入完全覆盖区域（阶段 3，红色），额外示例产生递减的可吸收预测信息。

full.sec: 5.5 Format Learning: Early Transients Versus Capability Acquisition
full.secZh: 5.5 格式学习：早期瞬态与能力获取

items.en: Many tasks have two components: format (how to structure outputs) and capability (what to predict). Learning proceeds differently for these components, creating artifacts in early training that must be distinguished from capability acquisition.
items.zh: 许多任务有两个组成部分：格式（如何结构化输出）和能力（预测什么）。这些组件的学习过程不同，在早期训练中会产生必须与能力获取区分开的人为现象。

items.en: Consider a task where the model must output answers in a specific format (e.g., “The answer is X” rather than just “X”). Format is low-entropy and quickly learned, whereas capability is high-entropy and requires more data. A model fine-tuned on such a task shows rapid initial loss reduction as format is acquired, followed by slower improvement as capability develops.
items.zh: 考虑一个任务，模型必须以特定格式输出答案（例如，“答案是 X”而不是仅仅“X”）。格式是低熵的且学习迅速，而能力是高熵的且需要更多数据。在此类任务上微调的模型显示出快速的初始损失下降（格式被获取），随后是较慢的改进（能力发展）。

items.en: The evolution of the EDL trajectory during training reflects this structure. Early training accumulates MDL rapidly (format not yet learned, so outputs are surprising) but also sees rapid test loss improvement (format quickly acquired). This yields an initial spike in EDL followed by decreasing per-example returns to EDL despite significant training. As format learning completes, subsequent examples address capability. MDL accumulation may slow (format already known) or continue at a moderate rate (capability still developing), while test loss improvement also slows. EDL per example may increase during this phase as capability develops.
items.zh: 训练期间 EDL 轨迹的演变反映了这种结构。早期训练快速累积 MDL（格式尚未学习，因此输出令人惊讶），但也看到测试损失快速改善（格式迅速获取）。这导致 EDL 初始峰值，随后每示例 EDL 回报递减，尽管训练显著。随着格式学习完成，后续示例处理能力。MDL 累积可能减慢（格式已知）或以中等速率继续（能力仍在发展），而测试损失改进也减慢。在此阶段，每示例 EDL 可能随着能力发展而增加。

items.en: When task requires both format and capability learning, EDL per example may initially decrease (format learning) before increasing (capability learning) or stabilizing (capability saturation).
items.zh: 当任务需要同时学习格式和能力时，每示例 EDL 可能先下降（格式学习）再上升（能力学习）或稳定（能力饱和）。

items.en: This model motivates the practical recommendation to score only designated output tokens (e.g., answer tokens) or normalize outputs when computing EDL for capability analysis. Scoring the full or unnormalized output, including format elements, confounds format learning with capability acquisition and can obscure important signatures of interest.
items.zh: 该模型支持实际建议：在计算 EDL 进行能力分析时，仅对指定的输出 token（例如，答案 token）进行评分或对输出进行归一化。对完整或未归一化的输出（包括格式元素）进行评分会混淆格式学习与能力获取，并可能掩盖重要的感兴趣特征。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Format learning can induce transients in EDL scaling and dynamical behavior. In teaching scenarios, format learning can cause a transient drop in the per-example EDL (EDL/n\text{EDL}/n), as format comprises generalizable structure that can be easily learned before task capability is acquired.
items.capZh: 图 3：格式学习可以在 EDL 缩放和动态行为中引起瞬态。在教学场景中，格式学习可能导致每示例 EDL（EDL/n\text{EDL}/n）的瞬态下降，因为格式包含可泛化结构，可以在任务能力获取之前轻松学习。

items.en: This section addresses predictable confusions about the interpretation of EDL and its relationship to other notions of information. Deconflating these perspectives and clarifying their subtleties is essential for correctly interpreting empirical measurements.
items.zh: 本节讨论关于 EDL 解释及其与其他信息概念关系的可预测混淆。厘清这些视角并阐明其细微差别对于正确解读经验测量至关重要。

full.secZh: 6.1 计算量 vs 分布期望

items.en: The prequential MDL is computed on a specific realized sequence of training examples D={(xi,yi)}i=1nD=\{(x_{i},y_{i})\}_{i=1}^{n}, and describes the amount of information in those data via the minimum compressed file size necessary to optimally encode them with the chosen learning algorithm. However, in what sense does this computed quantity represent “information”?
items.zh: 序贯 MDL 是在特定的训练示例序列 D={(xi,yi)}i=1nD=\{(x_{i},y_{i})\}_{i=1}^{n} 上计算的，并通过使用所选学习算法最优编码这些数据所需的最小压缩文件大小来描述这些数据中的信息量。然而，这个计算出的量在何种意义上代表“信息”？

items.en: Information-theoretic quantities such as entropy and mutual information are defined as expectations over distributions. The entropy of a random variable YY is H​(Y)=𝔼​[−log⁡P​(Y)]H(Y)=\mathbb{E}[-\log P(Y)], an expected value; it is not a property of any particular realization of the outputs of some data-generating procedure. Similarly, the “information” conveyed by observing a specific value yy is typically defined through the expected reduction in uncertainty conditional on that observation.
items.zh: 诸如熵和互信息等信息论量被定义为对分布的期望。随机变量 YY 的熵为 H​(Y)=𝔼​[−log⁡P​(Y)]H(Y)=\mathbb{E}[-\log P(Y)]，是一个期望值；它不是某个数据生成过程输出的任何特定实现的属性。类似地，观察特定值 yy 所传达的“信息”通常通过该观察条件下不确定性的期望减少来定义。

items.en: The prequential MDL is a sum of realized losses, not expected losses. For a single dataset, it is a random variable depending on the specific examples that were drawn and the order in which they were encoded with the model. Its interpretation as the “information content of the data” is most rigorous when considering the expectation over datasets: 𝔼D​[MDL​(D;θ0,A)]\mathbb{E}_{D}[\text{MDL}(D;\theta_{0},A)] measures the expected codelength of a sequence of data sampled from the distribution and has clear information-theoretic meaning in a source coding sense.
items.zh: 预序 MDL 是实际损失的总和，而非期望损失。对于单个数据集，它是一个随机变量，取决于抽取的具体示例以及它们用模型编码的顺序。将其解释为“数据的信息内容”在考虑对数据集的期望时最为严格：𝔼D​[MDL​(D;θ0,A)]\mathbb{E}_{D}[\text{MDL}(D;\theta_{0},A)] 衡量从分布中采样的数据序列的期望编码长度，并在源编码意义上具有清晰的信息论含义。

items.en: For a single computed EDL value, the appropriate interpretation is as an estimate of the expected generalizable information a model can gain from the data during training. Just as a sample mean estimates a population mean, the computed EDL estimates the expected information that would be absorbed across datasets drawn from 𝒟\mathcal{D}. Variance across datasets (or across random orderings of a fixed dataset) provides a measure of the uncertainty in this estimate.
items.zh: 对于单个计算的 EDL 值，适当的解释是作为模型在训练期间可以从数据中获得的期望可泛化信息的估计。正如样本均值估计总体均值，计算的 EDL 估计从分布 𝒟\mathcal{D 中抽取的数据集所吸收的期望信息。跨数据集（或固定数据集的随机排序）的方差提供了该估计不确定性的度量。

items.en: This perspective suggests reporting confidence intervals or variance estimates alongside EDL measurements, particularly for small datasets where variance may be substantial.
items.zh: 这一观点建议在 EDL 测量中报告置信区间或方差估计，特别是对于方差可能较大的小数据集。

full.sec: 6.2 One Label Can Provide Many Bits
full.secZh: 6.2 一个标签可提供许多比特

items.en: A binary label has Shannon entropy at most 1 bit. How, then, can a single example provide more than 1 bit of information to a learner?
items.zh: 一个二元标签的香农熵最多为 1 比特。那么，单个示例如何能为学习器提供超过 1 比特的信息？

items.en: Here, the resolution lies in distinguishing the entropy of the label itself from the information the label provides about the learning problem (i.e., data distribution). The label’s entropy measures uncertainty about the individual label itself, in isolation. The information about the learning problem measures uncertainty reduction about which hypothesis (or parameter setting) governs the data.
items.zh: 这里，解决之道在于区分标签本身的熵与标签关于学习问题（即数据分布）所提供的信息。标签的熵衡量的是对单个标签本身的不确定性，是孤立的。关于学习问题的信息衡量的是关于哪个假设（或参数设置）支配数据的不确定性的减少。

items.en: When a learner has mm hypotheses under consideration, eliminating all but a single hypothesis provides log⁡m\log m bits of information about which hypothesis is correct, regardless of the label’s own entropy. This information (what is specifically gained about the overall distribution by eliminating hypotheses) is what EDL captures and represents: the reduction in the model’s uncertainty about how to predict future examples.
items.zh: 当学习器考虑 mm 个假设时，消除除一个之外的所有假设提供了 log⁡m\log m 比特关于哪个假设正确的信息，无论标签自身的熵如何。这种信息（通过消除假设具体获得的关于整体分布的信息）正是 EDL 所捕获和代表的：模型关于如何预测未来示例的不确定性的减少。

items.en: In formal terms, consider the mutual information between the label YY and the hypothesis hh governing the data: I​(Y;h)I(Y;h). This can be as large as H​(h)=log⁡mH(h)=\log m, even when H​(Y)H(Y) is small. The label may have low marginal entropy but high mutual information with the hypothesis.
items.zh: 用形式化术语，考虑标签 YY 与支配数据的假设 hh 之间的互信息：I​(Y;h)I(Y;h)。即使 H​(Y)H(Y) 很小，它也可以大到 H​(h)=log⁡mH(h)=\log m。标签可能具有低边际熵，但与假设具有高互信息。

items.en: This clarifies how elicitation can occur with minimal data. If the model’s uncertainty is over which of its latent capabilities to apply (a small hypothesis space, as the model already has the requisite capability), a single illustrative example can resolve this uncertainty entirely. The example provides many bits of information about how to respond to the task, not by conveying information about the label itself, but by identifying which latent competency is relevant.333EDL quantifies the amount of information specifically present in the train dataset that was gained by the model and used to determine how to properly respond to the task. As such, the maximum amount of this generalizable information that a model can directly extract from the train data is the total information content of the train set itself.
items.zh: 这阐明了如何用最少的数据实现激发。如果模型的不确定性在于应用其哪个潜在能力（一个小的假设空间，因为模型已经具备必要能力），一个说明性的示例可以完全解决这种不确定性。该示例提供了关于如何响应任务的许多比特信息，不是通过传达关于标签本身的信息，而是通过识别哪个潜在能力是相关的。333EDL 量化了训练数据集中具体存在的、被模型获得并用于确定如何正确响应任务的信息量。因此，模型可以直接从训练数据中提取的最大可泛化信息量是训练集本身的总信息含量。

items.en: EDL measures predictive compression, i.e., how much better the final model predicts test labels compared to encoding labels sequentially using the continually updating model being trained. This is a statistical notion of information, rather than a semantic one.
items.zh: EDL 衡量预测压缩，即最终模型预测测试标签的能力比使用不断更新的训练模型顺序编码标签好多少。这是一个统计意义上的信息概念，而非语义上的。

items.en: A model with high EDL is better at predicting test labels. This improved prediction may arise from “understanding” the task in some meaningful sense, or it may arise from pattern matching, memorization, or exploitation of statistical regularities. However, EDL is agnostic to the mechanism by which this is accomplished; it measures the predictive improvement but does not reveal how that improvement was achieved.
items.zh: 具有高 EDL 的模型能更好地预测测试标签。这种改进的预测可能源于对任务的某种有意义“理解”，也可能源于模式匹配、记忆或统计规律利用。然而，EDL 对实现这一改进的机制不可知；它衡量预测改进，但不揭示改进是如何实现的。

items.en: Similarly, EDL does not measure “knowledge” in any philosophical sense. A model might absorb information which is sufficient to predict labels correctly, while having no meaningful representation of the underlying concepts. Conversely, a model might “understand” a domain deeply but show low EDL if that understanding was already evident before fine-tuning (elicitation or refinement of a demonstrated capability) or if the test distribution differs from training (distribution shift).
items.zh: 类似地，EDL 不以任何哲学意义衡量“知识”。模型可能吸收足以正确预测标签的信息，同时没有对底层概念的有意义表示。相反，模型可能深入“理解”一个领域，但如果这种理解在微调前已经明显（激发或精炼已展示的能力），或者测试分布与训练不同（分布偏移），则可能显示低 EDL。

items.en: These distinctions matter for interpreting EDL measurements. It is important to remember that EDL reflects the change in a model’s ability to identify regularities in the data and to make better predictions based on this recognition. High EDL indicates that fine-tuning has substantially improved predictions, but it does not reveal whether the improvement reflects genuine capability acquisition or more superficial explanations. Low EDL in an elicitation scenario indicates that the capability was largely manifest beforehand, but it does not reveal whether the capability reflects “true understanding” or sophisticated pattern matching.
items.zh: 这些区别对于解释 EDL 测量很重要。重要的是要记住，EDL 反映了模型识别数据中规律性并基于这种识别做出更好预测的能力的变化。高 EDL 表明微调显著改进了预测，但不揭示改进是反映了真正的能力获取还是更表面的解释。在激发场景中低 EDL 表明能力在很大程度上事先已经显现，但不揭示该能力是反映了“真正理解”还是复杂的模式匹配。

items.en: Ultimately, EDL is a tool for measuring information flow during training, as opposed to one for revealing the nature of model representations.
items.zh: 最终，EDL 是衡量训练期间信息流的工具，而非揭示模型表示本质的工具。

items.en: A natural question is how EDL relates to the number of parameters in the model, particularly for parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods that update only a small (sub)set of parameters.
items.zh: 一个自然的问题是 EDL 如何与模型参数数量相关，特别是对于仅更新一小部分参数的高效微调方法。

items.en: Each parameter, represented in floating-point precision, has a theoretical upper bound on information content determined by its bit-length (e.g., 32 bits for float32). However, this bound is rarely approached in practice. The effective information capacity of a parameter depends on how it influences predictions, which is constrained by the loss landscape, optimization dynamics, and the model’s architecture.
items.zh: 每个参数以浮点精度表示，其信息含量有由位长决定的理论上界（例如，float32 为 32 比特）。然而，这个界在实践中很少达到。参数的有效信息容量取决于它如何影响预测，这受损失景观、优化动态和模型架构的约束。

items.en: EDL per trainable parameter, EDLparam=EDL/P\text{EDL}_{\text{param}}=\text{EDL}/P, where PP is the number of trainable parameters, provides an empirical measure of how much additional predictive information has been gained per parameter during training. For full fine-tuning with many parameters, this ratio is typically small; models tend to be over-parameterized, and many parameters may not store task-specific information. For parameter-efficient methods like LoRA with limited trainable parameters, this ratio can be larger, potentially approaching or exceeding 1 bit per parameter.
items.zh: 每个可训练参数的 EDL，EDLparam=EDL/P\text{EDL}_{\text{param}}=\text{EDL}/P，其中 PP 是可训练参数数量，提供了训练期间每个参数获得了多少额外预测信息的经验度量。对于具有许多参数的全微调，该比率通常很小；模型往往过参数化，许多参数可能不存储任务特定信息。对于像 LoRA 这样可训练参数有限的高效方法，该比率可能更大，可能接近或超过每参数 1 比特。

items.en: Empirically, we observe a threshold in bits per trainable parameter beyond which parameter-efficient methods degrade relative to full fine-tuning. When the task requires storing more information than the adapter’s parameters can accommodate, performance suffers. This parameter capacity threshold differs systematically between elicitation (lower threshold, ~0.01–0.1 bits/parameter) and teaching (higher threshold, ~1+ bits/parameter), reflecting the different information requirements of these regimes (see companion empirical paper).
items.zh: 经验上，我们观察到每可训练参数比特的阈值，超过该阈值高效方法相对于全微调性能下降。当任务需要存储的信息超过适配器参数所能容纳时，性能受损。这个参数容量阈值在激发（较低阈值，约 0.01–0.1 比特/参数）和教导（较高阈值，约 1+比特/参数）之间系统性地不同，反映了这些机制不同的信息需求（见配套实证论文）。

items.en: These thresholds are empirical observations; the theoretical basis for such thresholds, and their precise values, remain open questions (see subsection 7.3). The two-order-of-magnitude difference between regimes suggests a fundamental distinction in information requirements, which we formalize through the toy models in section 5. The connection between EDL per parameter and adapter capacity provides a promising direction for understanding the scaling behavior of parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
items.zh: 这些阈值是经验观察；这些阈值的理论基础及其精确值仍是开放问题（见第 7.3 节）。两种机制之间两个数量级的差异表明信息需求存在根本区别，我们通过第 5 节的玩具模型将其形式化。EDL 每参数与适配器容量之间的联系为理解高效微调的缩放行为提供了有希望的方向。

items.en: We have developed a formal framework for measuring the generalizable portion of train set information that fine-tuning absorbs into model parameters. Excess Description Length, defined via prequential coding, provides an operational quantity computable from standard training logs. We established that EDL is non-negative in expectation, converges to surplus description length asymptotically, and provides bounds on expected generalization improvement. Through toy models, we clarified when EDL signatures indicate elicitation (pre-existing capability; few bits needed) versus teaching (capability initially absent; many bits needed).
items.zh: 我们开发了一个形式化框架，用于衡量微调吸收到模型参数中的训练集信息的可泛化部分。通过预序编码定义的超额描述长度提供了一个可从标准训练日志计算的操作量。我们证明了 EDL 在期望上非负，渐近收敛于剩余描述长度，并为期望泛化改进提供了界。通过玩具模型，我们阐明了何时 EDL 特征指示激发（预先存在的能力；需要少量比特）与教导（初始缺乏能力；需要大量比特）。

items.en: The framework developed here provides foundations for empirical investigation of teaching versus elicitation. These learning regimes are characterized by qualitatively different EDL behavior:
items.zh: 本文开发的框架为实证研究教学与激发提供了基础。这些学习机制以定性不同的 EDL 行为为特征：

items.en: For elicitation, EDL per example monotonically decreases with dataset size (diminishing marginal generalizable information gained from additional examples), total EDL is small relative to capability improvement (capability was readily accessible or mostly present), and the bits-per-parameter threshold for adapter degradation relative to full fine-tuning is low (little new or remaining structure in the data to encode).
items.zh: 对于激发，每个样本的 EDL 随数据集大小单调递减（从额外样本中获得的边际可泛化信息递减），总 EDL 相对于能力提升较小（能力易于获取或已大部分存在），并且相对于全微调，适配器退化的每参数比特阈值较低（数据中几乎没有新结构或剩余结构需要编码）。

items.en: For teaching, EDL per example may increase during learning (new capability and associated representations being formed), total EDL is large relative to capability improvement (new structure being encoded in the model parameters), and the bits-per-parameter threshold is higher (substantial new structure required).
items.zh: 对于教学，每个样本的 EDL 在学习过程中可能增加（正在形成新能力及相关表示），总 EDL 相对于能力提升较大（新结构正在被编码到模型参数中），并且每参数比特阈值较高（需要大量新结构）。

items.en: These signatures, formalized through our framework, provide testable predictions for empirical study. Causal interventions, such as pre-training on related tasks before measuring EDL on a target task, can shift EDL signatures from teaching-like to elicitation-like (shifting capacity thresholds by more than an order of magnitude and inverting EDL scaling behavior), providing evidence for the distinction of latent versus absent capabilities.
items.zh: 这些通过我们的框架形式化的特征为实证研究提供了可检验的预测。因果干预，例如在目标任务上测量 EDL 之前对相关任务进行预训练，可以将 EDL 特征从教学型转变为激发型（将容量阈值改变一个数量级以上并反转 EDL 缩放行为），为潜在能力与缺失能力的区分提供了证据。

items.en: The framework developed here makes specific predictions that we validate empirically in a companion paper:
items.zh: 本文开发的框架做出了具体预测，我们在配套论文中通过实证验证：

items.en: EDL scaling signatures: Elicitation shows monotonically decreasing EDL/token with dataset size; teaching shows an initial increasing phase. These signatures are robust across models (Llama 3 1B–8B, TinyStories-1B, Qwen2.5 1.5B–14B) and tasks (arithmetic, reasoning).
items.zh: EDL 缩放特征：激发显示每个 token 的 EDL 随数据集大小单调递减；教学显示初始递增阶段。这些特征在模型（Llama 3 1B–8B、TinyStories-1B、Qwen2.5 1.5B–14B）和任务（算术、推理）上具有鲁棒性。

items.en: Causal intervention: Pre-teaching a skill (e.g., multiplication via operator notation) converts a teaching task to an elicitation task, reducing information thresholds by ~10–100x.
items.zh: 因果干预：预先教授一项技能（例如通过运算符表示法进行乘法）将教学任务转换为激发任务，将信息阈值降低约 10–100 倍。

items.en: Random label control: EDL collapses to near zero when labels are randomly permuted, confirming that EDL measures learnable structure rather than training effort.
items.zh: 随机标签控制：当标签被随机排列时，EDL 降至接近零，确认 EDL 衡量的是可学习结构而非训练努力。

items.en: Parameter capacity prediction: Performance degrades significantly when EDL per parameter exceeds regime-dependent thresholds, with elicitation thresholds (~0.01–0.1 bits/parameter) roughly 100x lower than teaching thresholds (>1 bit/parameter).
items.zh: 参数容量预测：当每参数 EDL 超过依赖于机制的阈值时，性能显著下降，激发阈值（约 0.01–0.1 比特/参数）比教学阈值（>1 比特/参数）低约 100 倍。

items.en: These empirical results support the theoretical framework we introduce here and demonstrate practical utility for predicting fine-tuning behavior.
items.zh: 这些实证结果支持了我们在此引入的理论框架，并展示了预测微调行为的实际效用。

items.en: Several limitations of our framework deserve acknowledgment.
items.zh: 我们的框架存在若干局限性值得承认。

items.en: First, EDL depends on the training algorithm. This dependence is real and meaningful—different algorithms genuinely differ in learning efficiency—but this complicates comparison across settings. The choice of algorithm should be held fixed when comparing EDL across models or tasks.
items.zh: 首先，EDL 依赖于训练算法。这种依赖是真实且有意义的——不同算法在学习效率上确实存在差异——但这使得跨设置比较变得复杂。在跨模型或任务比较 EDL 时，应保持算法选择固定。

items.en: Second, EDL is not a semantic measure of knowledge or understanding. Large EDL indicates improved prediction but does not reveal how improvement was achieved. Models with similar EDL may have very different internal representations.
items.zh: 其次，EDL 不是知识或理解的语义度量。大的 EDL 表示预测改进，但不揭示改进是如何实现的。具有相似 EDL 的模型可能具有非常不同的内部表示。

items.en: Third, our analysis focuses on supervised fine-tuning. Extensions to reinforcement learning, preference optimization, and other post-training methods require additional development.
items.zh: 第三，我们的分析侧重于监督微调。扩展到强化学习、偏好优化和其他后训练方法需要进一步开发。

items.en: Fourth, the toy models, while illuminating, are highly simplified. Real learning involves continuous hypothesis spaces, complex coverage dynamics, and entanglement of format and capability that our discrete toy models do not fully capture.
items.zh: 第四，玩具模型虽然具有启发性，但高度简化。真实学习涉及连续假设空间、复杂的覆盖动态以及格式与能力的纠缠，我们的离散玩具模型未能完全捕捉。

items.en: The EDL formalism we introduce here provides a preliminary framework for assessing capability emergence and describing different learning regimes. There are several directions and open questions which we believe merit further investigation.
items.zh: 我们在此引入的 EDL 形式化为评估能力涌现和描述不同学习机制提供了初步框架。有几个方向和开放问题我们认为值得进一步研究。

items.en: Can we establish tighter bounds relating EDL to generalization? Our current bounds are loose; sharper characterization would strengthen the framework.
items.zh: 我们能否建立将 EDL 与泛化联系起来的更紧界？我们当前的界是松的；更精确的表征将增强框架。

items.en: How does information distribute across model components during fine-tuning? Layer-wise or module-wise analysis of EDL accumulation could reveal where capability information is stored.
items.zh: 在微调过程中，信息如何在模型组件间分布？逐层或逐模块的 EDL 累积分析可以揭示能力信息的存储位置。

items.en: Can EDL scaling reliably predict capability ceilings? If the trajectory of EDL with dataset size has predictable asymptotic behavior, it may be possible to estimate maximum achievable performance from early training data.
items.zh: EDL 缩放能否可靠地预测能力上限？如果 EDL 随数据集大小的轨迹具有可预测的渐近行为，则可能从早期训练数据估计最大可实现性能。

items.en: What training algorithms optimize information extraction? If EDL measures absorbed information, are there algorithms that maximize EDL (and thus learning efficiency) for a given data budget?
items.zh: 哪些训练算法能优化信息提取？如果 EDL 衡量吸收的信息，是否存在在给定数据预算下最大化 EDL（从而最大化学习效率）的算法？

items.en: Our methodology is grounded in an information-theoretic view of learning, using the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle to quantify the information models absorb from data (Rissanen, 1978; Dawid, 1984; Barron et al., 1998). This approach is motivated by prior work in information-theoretic probing. Notably, Blier and Ollivier (2018) and Voita and Titov (2020) also decomposed the MDL of a dataset into a model component and a data component, finding that the model component reflects the information captured by the parameters after training. Their formulation is a conceptual precursor to our definition of Excess Description Length (EDL).
items.zh: 我们的方法基于学习的信息论视角，使用最小描述长度（MDL）原理来量化模型从数据中吸收的信息（Rissanen, 1978; Dawid, 1984; Barron et al., 1998）。该方法受到信息论探针方面先前工作的启发。值得注意的是，Blier 和 Ollivier（2018）以及 Voita 和 Titov（2020）也将数据集的 MDL 分解为模型组件和数据组件，发现模型组件反映了训练后参数捕获的信息。他们的公式是我们定义超额描述长度（EDL）的概念前身。

items.en: Our framework also builds upon the concept of Surplus Description Length (SDL) (Whitney et al., 2021), a powerful, information-theoretic measure of the quality of learned representations. However, SDL is typically defined in an asymptotic, infinite-data setting. A central contribution of our work is to formulate EDL as a practical, finite-data analog to SDL. While EDL can converge to SDL in the infinite-data limit, its primary advantage is in analyzing the realistic, resource-constrained scenarios common in fine-tuning. EDL is designed to answer practical questions about how far a small, finite amount of information can be stretched, providing a tool to quantify learning dynamics where data is scarce. This allows us to connect our formal metrics to an intuitive understanding of elicitation and teaching in a way that asymptotic measures cannot.
items.zh: 我们的框架还建立在剩余描述长度（SDL）的概念之上（Whitney et al., 2021），这是一种强大的、信息论的学习表示质量度量。然而，SDL 通常是在渐近的无限数据设置中定义的。我们工作的一个核心贡献是将 EDL 表述为 SDL 的实用有限数据类比。虽然 EDL 在无限数据极限下可以收敛到 SDL，但其主要优势在于分析微调中常见的现实资源受限场景。EDL 旨在回答关于少量有限信息能被拉伸多远这一实际问题，提供了一种在数据稀缺时量化学习动态的工具。这使我们能够将形式化指标与对激发和教学的直观理解联系起来，而渐近度量无法做到这一点。

items.en: Our framework connects to a rich literature on information-theoretic analysis of learning. Xu and Raginsky (2017) established generalization bounds based on the mutual information between training data and learned parameters, showing that algorithms with bounded information complexity generalize well. Steinke and Zakynthinou (2020) extended this analysis to adaptive data analysis, where queries depend on previous answers. The PAC-Bayes framework (McAllester, 1999; Catoni, 2007) provides generalization bounds based on the KL divergence between posterior and prior over hypotheses, which can be viewed as a description length. Our EDL framework differs in focusing on the prequential coding perspective, which provides operational semantics through the interactive communication protocol, as well as in emphasizing the finite-data regime where teaching and elicitation exhibit qualitatively different signatures.
items.zh: 我们的框架与关于学习的信息论分析的丰富文献相联系。Xu 和 Raginsky（2017）基于训练数据与学习参数之间的互信息建立了泛化界限，表明具有有界信息复杂度的算法泛化良好。Steinke 和 Zakynthinou（2020）将这一分析扩展到自适应数据分析，其中查询依赖于先前的答案。PAC-Bayes 框架（McAllester, 1999; Catoni, 2007）基于假设后验与先验之间的 KL 散度提供泛化界限，这可以视为一种描述长度。我们的 EDL 框架的不同之处在于关注前向编码视角，该视角通过交互式通信协议提供操作语义，并强调教学和激发表现出定性不同特征的有限数据机制。

items.en: Understanding how fine-tuning modifies language models, whether surfacing latent capabilities or imparting new ones, is essential for capability evaluation and safety. The framework developed here provides rigorous foundations for this investigation, offering operational definitions, proven properties, and conceptual clarifications. We hope this work enables more precise empirical study of the teaching-versus-elicitation distinction and contributes to our understanding of what language models learn from fine-tuning.
items.zh: 理解微调如何修改语言模型——无论是浮现潜在能力还是赋予新能力——对于能力评估和安全性至关重要。本文开发的框架为这一研究提供了严格的基础，提供了操作定义、已证明的性质和概念澄清。我们希望这项工作能够促进对教学与激发区别的更精确实证研究，并有助于理解语言模型从微调中学到了什么。

items.en: We thank John Schulman, Ethan Perez, Eric Easley, and Vivek Hebbar for helpful discussions.
items.zh: 我们感谢 John Schulman、Ethan Perez、Eric Easley 和 Vivek Hebbar 的有益讨论。

items.en: E.D. conceived the Excess Description Length (EDL) framework, developed the theoretical formulation and proofs, designed and analyzed the toy models, and wrote the manuscript. E.D. also designed and conducted the empirical experiments described in the companion empirical paper that motivated and validated the theoretical framework.
items.zh: E.D.构思了超额描述长度（EDL）框架，发展了理论公式和证明，设计并分析了玩具模型，并撰写了手稿。E.D.还设计并进行了配套实证论文中描述的经验实验，这些实验激励并验证了理论框架。

items.en: F.R. proposed the disjoint subdistributions setting. H.J. and F.R. provided feedback on the theoretical development and interpretation, drafts, and contributed to discussion of the framework and its implications. J.L. supervised the project and provided guidance and feedback throughout.
items.zh: F.R.提出了不相交子分布设置。H.J.和 F.R.对理论发展和解释、草稿提供了反馈，并参与了框架及其影响的讨论。J.L.监督了该项目，并全程提供了指导和反馈。
